projects.title: Willowdale Custom Home — 4,700 sq ft Modern Luxury | Vitalite
projects.description: Ongoing 4,700 sq ft Willowdale custom home by Vitalite: zoning review, permit drawings, engineering coordination, site management, inspections, and closeout.
projects.headline: Ongoing 4,700 sq ft modern detached rebuild in Willowdale, managed from zoning and permit drawings through construction closeout.
narrative: This ongoing Willowdale project is a 4,700 sq ft modern detached rebuild in one of North York's most active custom home markets. The project replaces older housing stock with a higher-performing family residence planned around long-term property value, open-concept living and a luxury exterior presence.

Vitalite manages the project as a connected design-build scope: zoning review, architectural and engineering coordination, building permit drawings, structural framing, mechanical systems, finish-trade coordination, municipal inspections and final closeout. The owner has one accountable team instead of separate parties handing off risk.

Willowdale custom home work depends on early control of lot coverage, setbacks, structural spans, HVAC routing, glazing, drainage and inspection sequencing. Vitalite addressed those items before the build moved to site so construction decisions support the approved drawings and budget direction.

projects.title: Preston Lake Stouffville 200 sq ft Single-Storey Addition | Vitalite
projects.description: A 200 sq ft single-storey addition in Preston Lake, Stouffville with heated crawl space and vaulted cathedral roof. Expands the dining and family room in classic American country style.
projects.headline: A precise single-storey addition that opens up the dining and family room without losing the home's country character.
narrative: This Preston Lake project adds 200 sq ft to an existing house, extending the dining area and family room into one connected space. The addition uses a vaulted cathedral-style roof and a heated crawl space below, finished in a classic American country style that ties cleanly back into the existing home.

Single-storey additions at this scale need precise foundation work, properly insulated heated crawl spaces, structural tie-ins to the existing house, and roof framing that transitions cleanly between old and new. Vitalite coordinates architectural drawings, structural engineering, building permit, foundation, framing, mechanical and finishing trades on one schedule.

Stouffville additions are reviewed under Whitchurch-Stouffville zoning rules, so setbacks, lot coverage, and exterior design continuity all get resolved at the permit drawing stage before site work begins.

projects.title: Lansdowne Toronto Multi-Unit Build with Laneway Suite | Vitalite
projects.description: A new multi-unit residential building in Lansdowne, Toronto. Each floor is a self-contained unit with independent access, plus a garage with laneway house at the rear.
projects.headline: A purpose-built multi-unit residential building paired with a laneway suite to maximize rental return on a single lot.
scope: Self-contained unit per floor with independent access
narrative: This Lansdowne project is a ground-up multi-unit residential build where every floor functions as a separate, self-contained living unit with its own access. A garage with a laneway suite at the rear adds another rentable unit on the same lot, maximizing investment return without overshadowing the streetscape.

Multi-unit projects in Toronto require careful work on zoning permissions, fire separation between units, egress, parking, servicing, and HVAC layout. Vitalite handles zoning review, permit drawings, structural and mechanical engineering coordination, and the construction sequence so each unit is delivered code-compliant and rent-ready.

Lansdowne sits in an area where Toronto's multiplex zoning reforms and laneway suite by-laws can stack into significant rental income. Property owners who plan early for fire separation, parking, and laneway access typically move faster through Committee of Adjustment.

projects.title: Erindale Mississauga Side-Split Vertical Addition + Backyard Extension | Vitalite
projects.description: Large-scale addition expanding an Erindale, Mississauga side-split from four storeys to six, plus a backyard extension. Trussed roof, vaulted foyer, aluminum alloy doors and windows.
projects.headline: A vertical addition that resets a side-split's presence while adding usable family space.
narrative: This Erindale project is a major expansion of an existing four-storey side-split — adding two more levels and a backyard extension to bring the home to six storeys total. A new trussed roof, a vaulted foyer, and aluminum alloy doors and windows reset the home's exterior presence on the street.

Vertical additions on side-split houses are structurally complex. Existing foundations, walls and floor systems must be analyzed before adding floors, and the roof system has to be redesigned around new load paths. Vitalite coordinates the structural engineering, architectural drawings, permit submission, demolition, framing, mechanical upgrades and finishes so every stage stays in sync.
Mississauga building permit reviews typically focus on lot coverage, height, grading, and structural sufficiency — all factors that get resolved during pre-construction, before any framing begins.

projects.title: York Toronto Lot Severance — Two New Semi-Detached Homes | Vitalite
projects.description: Coming 2026: a 38'x120' lot in York, Toronto severed into two parcels with two new semi-detached homes (2,100 sq ft each). Investment-focused infill project.
projects.headline: One lot, two homes — a clean infill play that turns an underutilized parcel into two new semi-detached residences.
scope: Two semi-detached homes built in parallel
2,100 sq ft gross floor area each
narrative: This 2026 project takes a single 38'x120' lot in York, Toronto and severs it into two equal parcels, each with a new semi-detached home of 2,100 sq ft gross floor area. It's a typical pattern of Toronto infill: turning one underutilized lot into two homes that fit the existing streetscape.

Severance projects move through the Committee of Adjustment first, then proceed to zoning compliance, architectural drawings, structural and mechanical coordination, and building permits for both new homes. Vitalite prepares the application package, handles drawings and engineering, and runs the construction schedule for both halves in parallel.
Lot severance work in Toronto often hinges on minimum lot frontage, parking, and fit with the surrounding character. Owners who run zoning review early have a much smoother path to approvals.

projects.title: Bedford Park Mixed-Use Vertical Addition + 5 Rental Apartments | Vitalite
projects.description: Coming 2026: a complex vertical expansion on a Bedford Park, North York mixed-use building. Two new floors and five rental apartments above existing commercial and residential.
projects.headline: Building rental density on top of an active mixed-use property without disrupting commercial cash flow.
scope: Existing commercial and residential remain in use
narrative: This Bedford Park project builds on an existing two-storey mixed-use property — commercial at grade and residential above — adding two more storeys and five rental apartments. It's one of the more complex types of construction we handle: live commercial below, occupied residential, and significant new vertical construction above.

Vertical additions over occupied buildings require detailed structural assessment, careful staging, and thorough coordination with tenants and the City of Toronto. Vitalite plans the engineering, fire separation between uses, mechanical and electrical risers, building permit drawings, and the construction sequence so the existing tenants can stay in place during most of the work.

Bedford Park sits along Yonge Street north of Lawrence — a corridor where mixed-use density is increasingly viable. Owners who add rental units the right way can build long-term value while keeping commercial cash flow.

projects.title: Avondale North York Custom Home — 3,200 sq ft Transitional Style | Vitalite
projects.description: Coming 2026: a 3,200 sq ft custom detached home in Avondale, North York with transitional styling that blends traditional warmth and contemporary design.
projects.primaryKeyword: Avondale North York custom home builder
projects.headline: A transitional-style custom home where detail execution carries the design weight.
narrative: This 2026 Avondale build is a 3,200 sq ft detached home with transitional styling — a deliberate blend of traditional warmth and contemporary clean lines. The exterior and interior are designed to be simple yet polished, with material details carrying most of the design weight.

Transitional builds depend heavily on detail execution: trim profiles, window proportions, finish transitions, and the way materials meet at corners. Vitalite coordinates the architectural drawings, finish selections, structural and mechanical engineering, building permit, and on-site quality control so the design intent survives construction.
Avondale is a North York area where buyers expect family-sized homes with strong long-term resale. A 3,200 sq ft detached on a well-planned lot fits that demand directly.

projects.title: Stouffville Retrofit + Multi-Space Expansion | Vitalite
projects.description: Coming 2026: a large-scale Stouffville home retrofit adding a front foyer, study, sunroom, and basement walk-up. Existing roof transformed into a cathedral-style ceiling.
projects.headline: A retrofit that adds usable rooms across the home and opens the upper floor with a cathedral ceiling.
narrative: This Stouffville project is a major retrofit on an existing home — adding a front foyer, a dedicated study, a sunroom, and a backyard basement walk-up staircase. The existing roof structure is also being transformed into a cathedral-style ceiling, opening the upper floor entirely.

Retrofits at this scope sit between a renovation and a custom home build. Vitalite handles structural analysis of the existing house, demolition planning, foundation extensions for the additions, basement walkout structural work, roof reframing for the cathedral ceiling, mechanical and electrical upgrades, and full interior finishes.

Stouffville projects fall under Whitchurch-Stouffville municipal building permit review. Lot grading, drainage, setbacks, and structural permits all need to be coordinated before the first wall comes down.

projects.title: Rural Stouffville Custom Home — American Country Style | Vitalite
projects.description: Coming 2026: a new detached custom home on the rural outskirts of Stouffville. Wood-grain siding, fluted panels, Hardie board, and stucco define the American country aesthetic.
projects.headline: A fresh take on American country style, executed with modern materials that hold up on a rural lot.
narrative: This Stouffville rural custom home is built around an American country-style aesthetic, executed with modern building materials. Wood-grain siding, fluted panels, Hardie board, and stucco are layered together to give the exterior a deliberate, fresh take on the country style without the maintenance burden of traditional cladding.

Rural builds in Whitchurch-Stouffville come with their own constraints — septic and well coordination, larger setbacks, road access, drainage, and occasionally conservation authority review. Vitalite plans these inputs into the design and permit drawings before the foundation is poured.

American country style works on a rural lot when the materials are chosen carefully and the proportions are honest. The cladding combination on this build is the design hinge — it's what carries the whole exterior.

projects.title: Willowdale Expansion — 3,700 sq ft Home Grown to 5,200 sq ft | Vitalite
projects.description: Coming 2026: a Willowdale, North York expansion that keeps the original foundation and adds 1,500 sq ft. Final home is 5,200 sq ft with 11-ft and 9.5-ft ceiling heights.
projects.headline: A foundation-preserving expansion that grows the home by 1,500 sq ft and adds significant ceiling height.
narrative: This Willowdale project takes a 3,700 sq ft home and expands it by 1,500 sq ft — keeping the original foundation intact while adding new framed area. The result is 5,200 sq ft of finished home with an 11-ft main-floor ceiling height and 9.5-ft on the second floor.

Expansions that retain the original foundation are a careful balance between cost savings and structural integrity. Vitalite assesses the existing foundation capacity, designs the new framing to load it correctly, raises the ceiling heights through structural beam upgrades, and re-coordinates the mechanical, electrical, and finishing trades around the larger volume.

Willowdale buyers often weigh expansion against full rebuild. Keeping the foundation can shorten the project timeline and reduce demolition and grading costs, but only when the existing structure can carry the new program.

projects.title: Completed Custom Home — Willowdale, North York | Vitalite
projects.description: A completed Vitalite custom home project in Willowdale, North York. Interior tours are no longer available; the project remains part of our portfolio history.
projects.headline: A past Willowdale custom home that informs how Vitalite plans new builds in the area today.
narrative: This Willowdale project was completed by Vitalite as part of our earlier custom home and rebuild work in North York. Willowdale is one of Toronto's most active rebuild markets — older bungalows and split-levels regularly being replaced with modern, family-scale custom homes.

Past projects in this neighbourhood have helped shape how Vitalite approaches zoning, design fit, and construction logistics on Willowdale lots. The interior is no longer accessible for visits, but the experience — its planning, structural work, permit coordination, and finish-stage management — informs how we deliver Willowdale projects today.
